message:
perf(mvcc): drop redundant SSI validations_total counter

`record_ssi_commit` and `record_ssi_abort` are called on every SSI
commit/abort and each was doing two unconditional relaxed `fetch_add`s
on process-wide counters. The second target,
`FSQLITE_SSI_VALIDATIONS_TOTAL`, was mathematically redundant: every
SSI validation resolves into exactly one `record_ssi_commit()` call or
exactly one `record_ssi_abort(_)` call, so

    validations_total = commits_total + aborts_total()

by definition. Drop the static, drop the second `fetch_add` from both
hot paths, and derive `validations_total` at snapshot time in
`ssi_metrics_snapshot()` (saturating to be defensive about wraparound
on the abort buckets). `reset_ssi_metrics` likewise drops the dead
store. The struct field stays on `SsiMetricsSnapshot` so external
consumers — currently `Connection::refresh_eprocess_oracle` via
`conflict_rate()` — see the same value as before.

There is a per-call consistency note: the old code already raced
between its first and second `fetch_add`, so any reader could observe
`commits_total + aborts_total > validations_total - 1` for a window.
The new code closes that race by definition (one read pass over four
counters always satisfies the identity at the moment of the snapshot)
while keeping the same multi-counter sampling skew that already
existed across `commits` vs `aborts`.

Microbench `bench_record_ssi_commit_validations_pruning` (4M cycles ×
9 trials, same-run baseline-vs-optimized via a local `AtomicU64`
stand-in for the deleted static):

    old (2 fetch_adds): median 3.91 ns/call
    new (1 fetch_add):  median 1.90 ns/call
    delta: -51.5%  (~2.06x faster)

The same shape applies to `record_ssi_abort` (one fewer `fetch_add`
per abort), but aborts are the cold path so the bench targets the
commit caller.

Correctness: 1345 pass / 7 fail / 22 ignored, identical to the
pre-existing concurrent-agent failure set noted in the prior cc_4
session memory (`begin_concurrent::test_zero_witness_commit_stays_…`,
`core_types::test_cleanup_orphaned_slots_logs_…`,
`history_compression::test_page_history_newest_full_image_…`,
`lifecycle::test_concurrent_same_page_writers_…`,
`lifecycle::test_publish_write_set_…`,
`lifecycle::test_write_skew_sum_constraint_…`,
`shared_lock_table::test_try_acquire_conflict_logs_…`). The six SSI
metrics tests (`ssi_metrics_commit_counting`,
`ssi_metrics_abort_by_reason`, `ssi_metrics_reset`,
`ssi_metrics_conflict_rate`, `ssi_metrics_conflict_rate_zero_validations`,
`ssi_metrics_display`) all still pass — they read `validations_total`
through `ssi_metrics_snapshot()` so the derivation is transparent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
